page: Microsoft Azure D4als_v6 is a compute-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Dalv6 server family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The instance features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a base clock speed of 3.7 GHz. Memory capacity is limited to 8.0 GB, equating to a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. This configuration does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, though it is premium storage capable.

Performance benchmarks reveal strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU, Geekbench single-core, and text processing scores placing in the top 25% of tested hardware. Multi-core performance, image processing, and cryptography tests rank in the average tier. However, memory performance is a notable bottleneck; the Passmark memory score falls into the bottom 10%, and read-only memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks are in the bottom 25%. Similarly, database operations and multi-threaded decompression show weaker performance due to the restricted memory allocation.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D4als_v6 offers a budget-friendly entry point for compute-heavy workloads that do not require large memory footprints. The low memory-to-vCPU ratio makes it less suitable for memory-intensive databases or large-scale LLM inference, as demonstrated by poor performance on medium-sized model prompt processing. It is best suited for single-threaded tasks, lightweight web serving, text processing, and basic utility workloads that leverage its strong single-core CPU performance without exhausting the 8.0 GB memory limit.
description: Microsoft Azure D4als_v6 is a compute-optimized virtual machine from the Dalv6 family, powered by a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, this instance lacks local storage and GPUs but supports premium storage. Benchmarks show strong single-core CPU and text processing performance, placing in the top 25%, while multi-core execution and cryptography perform at average levels. Conversely, memory bandwidth and database operations are weak, with the Passmark memory score falling in the bottom 10%. This resource profile makes the D4als_v6 a cost-efficient option for single-threaded tasks, lightweight web serving, and text processing, whereas memory-heavy databases and large-scale language model inference are constrained by the 2.0 GB per vCPU memory allocation.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D4als_v6 is a compute-optimized virtual machine featuring a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9V74 processor, 4 dedicated vCPUs, and 8.0 GB of RAM, delivering strong single-core performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D4als_v6 is an AMD EPYC-powered virtual machine with 4 vCPUs and 8.0 GB RAM, optimized for single-core compute tasks.
tagline: A compute-optimized virtual machine featuring strong single-core AMD EPYC performance and a lean memory profile for cost-efficient utility workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz with 4 dedicated vCPUs
8.0 GB of system memory providing a lean 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio
Strong single-core CPU and text processing benchmark performance
No local storage or GPU accelerators bundled with the instance
Best suited for lightweight web serving, text processing, and single-threaded tasks
